core_mechanism: rare-event probability estimation by adaptive multilevel splitting: a tiny tail probability is factored into a product of moderate conditional probabilities across a sequence of nested level sets, with samples that cross each successive threshold being resampled/cloned to keep the ensemble concentrated in the rare region
brief_reason: The abstract targets accurate estimation of arbitrarily small p-values (a rare-event tail probability) via an adaptive multilevel splitting Monte Carlo scheme, which is exactly the splitting-based decomposition of a small probability into staged conditional crossings of increasing thresholds.
